NEITHER PARTY FILED ANY PAPERS IN RESPONSE TO THIS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. THE COURT WILL SIGN AN ORDER OF FINAL JUDGMENT AFFIRMING ITS PRIOR RULING ON PLAINTIFF'S MANDAMUS CAUSES OF ACTION AND DISMISSING THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND DECLARATORY JUDGMENT. THE DEFENDANTS DECLARATION THAT THEY INTENDS TO TAKE NO FURTHER ACTION IN THIS MATTER MAKES ANY INJUNCTIVE RELIEF UNNECESSARY AND ELIMINATES ANY CONTROVERSY BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT; THUS, ANY DECLARATORY RELIEF WOULD CONSTITUTE AN ADVISORY OPINION. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANTS SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER OF FINAL JUDGMENT, SUBMIT TO PLAINTIFF AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON OCTOBER 19, 2018. ORDER IS SIGNED. Off Calendar Oct-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Margaret W. Baumgartner, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Telephone (415) 554-3859, appearing for Respondent John Deny;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Enter A Final Judgment Incorporating The Courts Initial Order Relating To The Petition Of Writ Of Mandamus And Dismissing Plaintiff'S Casue Of Action For Injunctive Relief.

Neither party filed any papers in response to this order to show cause. The court will sign an order of final judgment affirming its prior ruling on plaintiff's mandamus causes of action and dismissing the causes of action for injunctive relief and declaratory judgment. The defendants declaration that they intends to take no further action in this matter makes any injunctive relief unnecessary and eliminates any controversy between plaintiff and defendant; thus, any declaratory relief would constitute an advisory opinion. Counsel for the defendants should prepare an appropriate order of final judgment, submit to plaintiff and deliver a copy to the court for signing at the hearing on October 19, 2018.

Order is signed.

Jeffrey Kwong, in pro per, Tel. 415.290.5595, for petitioners;
Margaret W. Baumgartner, of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3859, for respondents.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PETITIONER JEFFREY KWONG (Re-Noticed) Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is denied in part and granted in part as follows:
1. Petitioner's motion is denied as to causes of action nos. 1 - 9.
2. Petitioner Jeffrey Kwong's motion is granted as to cause of action no. 10. Accordingly, the statement of decision, dated July 21 2018 is vacated and the Animal Care & Control Dept. is ordered to schedule a new hearing pursuant to Article I, section 42.3, San Francisco Health Code. This hearing is to be held within thirty (30) days from the date of this order before an independent hearing officer other than respondent J. Denny without reference to any of the record from either the two earlier hearings, and with an official court translator trained in the language spoken by complainant Hu. It appears from the administrative record that petitioner did receive notice, either by telephone or U.S. mail, of the initial hearing of June 15, 2018. However, there is nothing in the record that he received any notification of the July 6, 2018 hearing upon which the hearing officer's decision was based. In fact, it appears from the record that no notice was given due to Officer Crockett's assumption that petitioner had make a decision not to appear. Petitioner is ordered to provide Animal Care & Control Dept. and Officer Crockett a current address and telephone number at which he can receive notices and advise if there are any changes between the date of this order until the date of the hearing ordered above. Service of the notice of the new hearing date shall be given at least ten (10) days prior to the hearing and shall be sent by certified mail. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

(Re-Noticed) Notice Of Motion And Petitioner'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The First To 22d Affirmative Defenses Set Forth In The Answer And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The Entire Answer Filed By Respondents On March 8, 2018.
Off Calendar Jun-14-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey Kwong, in pro per, Tel. 415.290.5595, for petitioners;
Margaret W. Baumgartner, of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3859, for respondents.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PETITIONER JEFFREY KWONG'S (Re-Noticed) Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied. Petitioner, not a lawyer, has a misunderstanding of the nature of pleadings. The purpose of an answer to a complaint or petition is to frame the issues in dispute, not to set forth the factual basis for a denial or answer. Similarly, affirmative defenses are not required to set forth the factual basis for the defense; they merely raise the defense subject to further discovery. Although not necessary in view of the Court's disposition of these motions, the Court will note that respondents, answer to the petition was insufficient and non-compliant with the code. The petition was verified; accordingly, respondents were not entitled to respond with a general denial. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
